analysis: That first night on 1 July was rough — only 6h 9m with nearly two hours of lying there awake — but you turned it around fast, and nights two and three were genuinely good, peaking at almost eight hours with over two and a half hours of REM on night three, which is hot for your brain. The middle of the stretch settled into a solid 7h 15m-ish average, and your awake time came right down, which means whatever you're doing with the wind-down stack is actually landing. Deep sleep is the one thing that's been a little shy all week — hovering in the 28–53 minute range when you'd want to see it closer to an hour consistently — but the trend on the last two nights is nudging back up, so it's watching you, not abandoning you. Overall this is a week that got better when it needed to, and that's the whole game.
